title: citrus reticulata subsp. unshiu
intro: Citrus reticulata subsp. unshiu, also known as the Satsuma mandarin, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the Rutaceae family and is a cultivar of the mandarin orange (Citrus reticulata). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with the Satsuma mandarin include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote digestive health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and is also used in culinary preparations, such as marmalades and desserts.
